docs.title: Echo Study Guide 🫀
docs.text: Welcome to the ARDMS Adult Echocardiography Study Hub — your complete, level-based prep system built from the latest ASE & IAC 2025 standards.

docs.title: 🎯 How to Use This Study Guide
🩺 Step 1 — Start with Your Level
docs.text: Choose the path that matches your experience: - Beginner: Build a strong foundation — measurements, normal values, and image views. - Intermediate: Master Doppler, chamber quantification, and pathology correlations. - Advanced: Review stress echo, congenital heart disease, and board exam simulation.

docs.title: ⚙️ Step 2 — Explore by Topic
docs.text: Use the sidebar on the left to jump into any category. Every page includes: - Color-coded tables (🟢 Normal, 🟡 Borderline, 🔴 Abnormal) - Doppler reference charts - Example images and diagnostic tips

docs.title: 💡 Step 3 — Review & Practice
docs.text: At the end of each section: - Answer board-style questions. - Compare your responses to ASE criteria. - Track your progress as you advance levels.

docs.title: 🧭 Tip for Students
docs.text:
Bookmark this site or install it as a Progressive Web App (PWA) for offline review. Works perfectly on phones and tablets.

Built with ❤️ using MkDocs Material

docs.title: Doppler and Hemodynamics
docs.text: (ASE-verified 2025)

docs.title: Mitral Inflow – Diastolic Assessment
docs.text: Parameter Normal Mild Abnormal Severe Units Source E/A ratio 1.0–2.0 <0.8 or >2.0 >2.5 — [fact] ASE 2025 E/e′ avg (sept+lat) <10 10–14 >14 — [fact] ASE 2025 LAVI <34 35–40 >40 mL/m² [fact] ASE 2025 TR jet velocity <2.8 2.9–3.4 ≥3.5 m/s [fact] ASE 2025 DT (E wave) 160–240 <150 <130 = restrictive ms [fact] ASE 2025

docs.title: LVOT & Aortic Valve Hemodynamics
docs.text:
Variable Normal Moderate Severe Units Source LVOT diameter 1.8–2.3 — — cm [fact] ASE 2024 LVOT VTI 18–22 — <16 = low flow cm [fact] ASE 2024 Ao peak velocity <2.0 3.0–3.9 ≥4.0 m/s [fact] ASE 2024 Mean gradient <20 20–40 ≥40 mmHg [fact] ASE 2024 AVA (cont. eqn) >2.0 1.0–2.0 <1.0 cm² [fact] ASE 2024

Formula: [AVA = (LVOT area × LVOT VTI) / AV VTI]

docs.title: Right-Heart and Pulmonary Hemodynamics
docs.text: Parameter Normal Borderline Abnormal Units Source TR Vmax ≤2.8 2.9–3.4 ≥3.5 m/s [fact] ASE 2025 RH Guideline RVSP (4V² + RAP) <35 35–49 ≥50 mmHg [fact] ASE 2025 TAPSE ≥1.7 1.4–1.6 <1.4 cm [fact] ASE 2025 RV S′ (TDI) ≥9.5 8–9.4 <8 cm/s [fact] ASE 2025 FAC ≥35 % 30–34 % <30 % % [fact] ASE 2025

docs.title: Shunt and Stroke Volume Calculations
docs.text:
LVOT Stroke Volume: [SV = π (r²) × VTI]

Qp/Qs ratio (for shunts): [Qp/Qs = (VTI_{PV} × Area_{PV}) / (VTI_{LVOT} × Area_{LVOT})]

1.3 → significant L→R shunt < 0.8 → R→L shunt (rare)

docs.title: Valve Regurgitation Reference (ASE 2025 Consensus)
docs.text: Valve Mild Moderate Severe Units Method MR EROA <0.20 0.20–0.39 ≥0.40 cm² PISA MR Reg Vol <30 30–59 ≥60 mL PISA AR EROA <0.10 0.10–0.29 ≥0.30 cm² PISA TR VC width <0.3 0.3–0.69 ≥0.7 cm Color Jet

docs.title: References
docs.text:
1. ASE 2025 Guideline: Left Ventricular Diastolic Function by Echocardiography (JASE Jul 2025).
  2. ASE 2025 Right Heart Guideline: Comprehensive Assessment of Right Heart Structure and Function (Mar 2025).
  3. IAC 2025 Adult Echocardiography Standards (2025 Apr Edition).

docs.title: Normal Echocardiographic Values
docs.text:
(ASE-verified, 2023–2024)

Measurement Men Women Units Source LVEDD 4.2–5.8 3.8–5.2 cm ASE 2023 LVESD 2.5–4.0 2.2–3.5 cm ASE 2023 IVS Thickness 0.6–1.0 0.6–0.9 cm ASE 2023 PW Thickness 0.6–1.0 0.6–0.9 cm ASE 2023 LV Mass Index ≤115 ≤95 g/m² ASE 2023 LVEF (Biplane Simpson) 52–72 54–74 % ASE 2023 LA Volume Index ≤34 ≤34 mL/m² ASE 2023 RV Basal Diameter ≤4.1 ≤4.1 cm ASE 2022 RV Mid Diameter ≤3.5 ≤3.5 cm ASE 2022 RV Long Axis ≤8.6 ≤8.6 cm ASE 2022 TAPSE ≥1.7 ≥1.7 cm ASE 2022 RA Area (A4C) ≤18 ≤18 cm² ASE 2022 Ao Root Diameter 2.0–3.7 2.0–3.7 cm ASE 2023

docs.title: Diastolic Function — Key Parameters (ASE 2024)
docs.text: Parameter Normal Abnormal Units E/A ratio 1.0–2.0 <0.8 or >2.0 — E/e′ (average) <10 >14 — LAVI <34 ≥34 mL/m² TR jet velocity <2.8 ≥2.8 m/s Deceleration Time 160–240 <140 (restrictive) ms

docs.title: Valve Reference — Mean Gradients (for Doppler Reference)
docs.text: Valve Mild Moderate Severe Units Aortic Stenosis <20 20–40 >40 mmHg Mitral Stenosis <5 5–10 >10 mmHg Pulmonic Stenosis <36 36–64 >64 mmHg Tricuspid Stenosis <2 2–6 >6 mmHg

docs.title: References
docs.text:
1. Lang RM et al. J Am Soc Echocardiogr. 2023; 36(2): 123-145.
  2. Nagueh SF et al. J Am Soc Echocardiogr. 2024; 37(5): 421-452.
  3. Rudski LG et al. J Am Soc Echocardiogr. 2022; 35(3): 295-317.

docs.title: Cardiac Pathologies
docs.text:
(ASE & IAC-Verified 2024–2025)

Quick Summary

This section covers the major cardiac pathologies evaluated in the ARDMS Adult Echocardiography exam, based on ASE 2024–2025 guidelines. Each table uses color-coded markers for normal (🟢), borderline (🟡), and abnormal (🔴) findings.

docs.title: 🫀 Left Ventricular Hypertrophy (LVH)
docs.text: Type IVS (cm) PW (cm) LV Mass Index (g/m²) Interpretation 🟢 Normal ≤1.0 ≤1.0 ≤115 (M), ≤95 (F) Normal geometry 🟡 Concentric >1.0 >1.0 Elevated Pressure overload (HTN) 🔴 Eccentric >1.0 Normal Elevated Volume overload (MR, AR)

docs.title: ⚡ Dilated Cardiomyopathy (DCM)
docs.text:
Key Echo Findings - LVEDD > 5.8 cm (men), > 5.2 cm (women) - LVEF < 40% - Global hypokinesis - Functional MR/TR

Doppler Pattern

🔴 Reduced dP/dt, ↓ stroke volume, prolonged IVRT

docs.title: 💢 Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM)
docs.text:
Feature Diagnostic Threshold Notes Septal thickness ≥15 mm (unexplained) Often asymmetric (>1.3:1 ratio) LVOT velocity >4.0 m/s SAM of MV + posterior jet Gradient ≥30 mmHg at rest / ≥50 mmHg w/ provocation Dynamic obstruction

Tip

HCM patients may normalize gradient with Valsalva or β-blockers.

docs.title: 🧊 Restrictive Cardiomyopathy
docs.text:
Echo Characteristics - Bi-atrial enlargement - Normal LV size, thickened myocardium - E/A > 2.0, E/e′ > 15 - Low tissue Doppler velocities

Differential | Cause | Distinguishing Clue | |--------|---------------------| | Amyloidosis | “Sparkling” myocardium, low voltage ECG | | Hemochromatosis | Iron overload, increased attenuation | | Radiation/chemo | Fibrosis, small LV cavity |

docs.title: 💧 Pericardial Effusion / Tamponade
docs.text:
Finding Mild Moderate Severe Effusion size <10 mm 10–20 mm >20 mm RA collapse None Early diastole Throughout diastole RV collapse None Early diastole Throughout diastole IVC <2.1 cm + collapse 2.1–2.5 >2.5 cm, ↓ collapse

Warning

Tamponade confirmed when RA + RV collapse + dilated IVC + exaggerated respiratory inflow changes coexist.

docs.title: 🫧 Summary Chart — Diagnostic Echo Red Flags
docs.text: Category Key Finding Color LV function LVEF < 40% 🔴 Diastolic E/e′ > 15 🔴 RV function TAPSE < 1.4 cm 🔴 Pulmonary pressure TR Vmax ≥ 3.5 m/s 🔴 Tamponade RA collapse 🔴

docs.title: 📚 References
docs.text:
1. ASE 2025: Comprehensive LV and Diastolic Function Guidelines
  2. ASE 2025: Right Heart Quantification Update
  3. IAC 2025: Adult Echocardiography Standards
  4. Lang RM et al. J Am Soc Echocardiogr. 2023; 36(2): 123–145.

docs.title: Intermediate Guide — Overview
docs.text: Level focus: application & interpretation. Builds on Beginner by adding advanced Doppler logic, right-heart evaluation, and case-based reasoning.

docs.title: What you’ll learn
docs.text:
• Integrate TR velocity (TRV) + supportive signs to grade PH probability.
  • Apply updated diastolic criteria (ASE 2025 update).
  • Work through case correlations (HCM / DCM / Restrictive / Pericardial disease).

All numeric cutoffs sourced from ASE Right-Heart in Adults 2025 and ASE LV Diastolic Function 2025 guidelines.

docs.title: 1️⃣ Right-Heart & Pulmonary Pressures (ASE 2025)
docs.text:
Parameter Normal / Key Cutoff ASE 2025 Notes TRV (m/s) < 2.8 Use TRV + supportive echo signs for PH probability assessment TAPSE (cm) ≥ 1.7 Frames RV systolic function severity FAC (%) ≥ 35 < 35 = abnormal RV function

ASE Reference: JASE Mar 2025 — “Guidelines for the Echocardiographic Assessment of the Right Heart in Adults.”

docs.title: 2️⃣ Diastolic Function Highlights (ASE 2025)
docs.text:
Measure Normal Elevated Filling Pressure Average E/e′ < 10 > 14 TRV (m/s) < 2.8 ≥ 2.8 → supportive for ↑ LA pressure

ASE Reference: JASE Jul 2025 — “Recommendations for the Evaluation of Left Ventricular Diastolic Function by Echocardiography.”

docs.title: Stroke Volume (LVOT)
docs.text:
[ SV = \pi \left(\frac{D_{LVOT}}{2}\right)^2 \times VTI_{LVOT} ] Measure LVOT diameter (mid-systole, PLAX) × LVOT VTI (AP5/3 ch).

ASE Methodology Ref: “Quantification Methods in Cardiac Chambers,” JASE 2022 + still endorsed 2025.

docs.title: RVSP (from TR jet)
docs.text: [ RVSP = 4(TRV)^2 + RAP ] RAP by IVC size/collapse per ASE Right-Heart 2025.

docs.title: Formulas
docs.text: Diastology Toolkit (Inputs used in 2025 Algorithm) E/A, DT, e′(septal + lateral), avg E/e′, LAVI, TRV → integrated to classify diastolic function & filling pressures.

docs.title: Case 1 — Pulmonary Hypertension Probability
docs.text:
• TRV = 3.3 m/s
  • TAPSE = 1.3 cm, FAC = 28%
  • RA area enlarged, IVC dilated ↓ collapse

Interpretation (ASE 2025): TRV 3.3 → moderate band + supportive signs = elevated PH probability. Report: “PH probability intermediate–high per ASE 2025 Right-Heart guideline.”

docs.title: Case 2 — Diastolic Dysfunction (HFpEF phenotype)
docs.text: • E/A = 2.2 E/e′ = 16 LAVI = 38 TRV = 2.9 m/s → Elevated LV filling pressure by ASE 2025 algorithm.

docs.title: Case 3 — RV Dysfunction Staging
docs.text: • TAPSE = 1.05 cm FAC = 23% S′ = 6.0 cm/s → Moderate–severe RV systolic dysfunction (per graded bands ASE 2025).
